artifacts.summary: 6-phase plan: declarative services.home-manager.autoExpire edit (safe now) plus gated one-time reclamation (expire-before-GC) and activation, with before/after store-size verification

next_steps: Run /implement 63 to execute the plan. Phases 1-2 (baseline capture + home.nix edit) are safe now; Phases 3-6 (reclamation, switch, verification) are GATED until the Lean build completes.

memory_candidates.content: On NixOS, the root-level nix.gc timer runs nix-collect-garbage as root and only scans /nix/var/nix/profiles and per-user/root; it never touches a user's home-manager generations under ~/.local/state/nix/profiles/. To reclaim that space, add services.home-manager.autoExpire (enable, timestamp="-30 days", frequency, store.cleanup=true, store.options) in home.nix, which runs `home-manager expire-generations` THEN `nix-collect-garbage` on a weekly user timer. For one-time manual reclamation the order is mandatory: expire-generations FIRST (removes generation symlinks acting as GC roots), then nix-collect-garbage; reversing it makes the GC a no-op for HM closures. The active generation is always protected via ~/.local/state/home-manager/gcroots/current-home.
